Taskin Ahmed will say third man almost caught this, but Greaves will explain him about the pose he held after playing the upper cut! Greaves is loving Taskin Ahmed's pace when Bangladesh's captain probably expects him to take it off to the batters. Greaves used the pace to his advantage and guides it just fine enough to beat the fielder

full_text: Taskin Ahmed to Chris Greaves, , pace and width to a bloke who connects well is a big no no. Greaves was camping on the back foot to execute this cut shot and it beat backward point and third man quite comfortably

Long-on settles for a simple catch. Handy innings ends.

This was there to be hit, was in the slot but in the eagerness to clear the fence, he goes a bit too hard on the loft. Skies it straight down the ground and long-on has enough time to get across. Is that the game? Should probably be. Near impossible for the Tigers to get home now.

full_text: Wheal to Nurul Hasan, Caught by MacLeod!! Top, top catch! They do take a lot of such catches these days but that doesn't take away the fact they are tough ones. Full ball, a tad too full and Nurul couldn't quite swing cleanly. Still got a fair amount of elevation and distance. Nearly clearing long-on but MacLeod had other ideas. Judged the catch well, realised the momentum might carry him across, lobbed it up, and went back to get it calmly. No fuss at all. Scotland continue to chip away.

full_text: Mark Watt to Afif Hossain, Caught by Davey!! The gamble works for Scotland! You'd think that bowling a left-arm spinner to the leftie is a big risk, particularly at the death. But Watt isn't your usual spinner either. He loves to bowl at the back-end and has taken out a huge wicket here. Cross-seamer, such clever thinking and Afif's slog gets way more elevation than distance. Easily taken at deep mid-wicket. The left-hander was looking in good touch, so this is a huge blow to Bangladesh's hopes.

full_text: Chris Greaves to Mushfiqur, Bowled!! ! It's his night and he is doing all the talking with his performances. The previous wicket was a bad ball but not this one. full_text: Chris Greaves to Shakib, Caught by MacLeod!! ! Why didn't you bowl him earlier Mr.Coetzer? Greaves strikes first ball and breaks the threatening partnership. Not a wicket-taking ball at all though, one of those juicy long-hops that wrist spinners often seem to get away with. The line is wider and Shakib wanted to pull it over mid-wicket. Never got the desired connection and skewed it high towards the deep. Long-on and deep mid-wicket converged, the latter judged it beautifully to take a fine running catch, inches close to the ropes. Right then, is this the opening Scotland were waiting for?

full_text: Davey to Shakib, 1 run, Coetzer had the time too but he fluffs the direct hit. The ball seems to be following him a lot over the last 10-15 minutes. What a moment this would have been though! Scotland badly needed to break this partnership. Shakib bunted this towards mid-off and called Mushfiqur across for the single. The all-rounder had given up on the run and was gone by yards if the throw hit

full_text: Davey to Shakib, no run, crafty bowling, off-cutter dug into the surface and Shakib was way early into the cross-batted swat. The line anyways made the shot tough and the lack of pace made it impossible

short_text: Wheal to Liton Das, Caught by Munsey!! It's two down. Pressure on Bangladesh. Now this is full credit to the bowler for
full_text: Wheal to Liton Das, Caught by Munsey!! It's two down. Pressure on Bangladesh. Now this is full credit to the bowler for watching the batter till the last moment. Already into the 4th over, so Liton Das wants to maximize the powerplay with not many runs scored. He runs at the bowler, but Wheal saw what Liton was trying and dropped the length short. Liton thought he could get a drive on the rise and clear the infield but he picked out mid-off. 18/2.

short_text: Davey to Soumya Sarkar, Caught by Munsey!! What planning and field placing from Scotland. Did Soumya Sarkar know they pu
full_text: Davey to Soumya Sarkar, Caught by Munsey!! What planning and field placing from Scotland. Did Soumya Sarkar know they put a man at deep mid-wicket? Davey came round the wicket for this full delivery and angled it into the pads with two men on the leg-side fence - one in front of square, one behind. Sarkar played the stylish flick with great wrist work, didn't bother keeping it down and suddenly seems to have realised "oh dear, there is a man there." Straight to the fielder and off he goes to the dressing room.
